bbc.title: Oil price jumps despite deal to release record amount of reserves
bbc.description: It comes as Iranian attacks on ships intensify in the crucial Strait of Hormuz waterway.

bbc.title: Bowen: Trump has called for an Iran uprising but the lessons from Iraq in 1991 loom large
bbc.description: The US president might learn that starting wars is much easier than ending them, writes the BBC's international editor.

bbc.title: Epstein used modelling agent to recruit girls, Brazilian women tell BBC
bbc.description: Modelling agent used businesses to recruit girls and arrange US visas to visit Jeffrey Epstein, Brazilian women tell BBC.

bbc.title: Noma head chef resigns from restaurant amid abuse allegations
bbc.description: René Redzepi had previously apologised after ex-employees of the Danish restaurant accused him of creating a toxic work environment.

bbc.title: War in Ukraine spills into Hungarian election campaign
bbc.description: Hungary is going to the polls soon and Prime Minister Viktor Orbán has one public enemy in his sights - Ukraine's president.

bbc.title: What did we learn from China's biggest political meeting?
bbc.description: BBC correspondents give us their biggest takeaways from China's National People's Congress.

bbc.description: The G7 group of nations welcomes the idea of releasing oil in response to the surge in prices since the US-Israel war with Iran began

bbc.title: Bodies of two Chinese backpackers found in Australian floodwaters
bbc.description: The pair had been reported missing on Tuesday after failing to reach their destination.

wsj.title: Palestinians Stream Back to Northern Gaza on Foot
wsj.description: Israel allowed displaced Gazans to begin crossing a military zone that bisects the enclave after a deadlock over hostage releases was broken.

wsj.title: Leading China Property Developer Reports Huge loss, in Sign of Widening Real-Estate Woes
wsj.description: Troubles at Vanke raise questions about the continued spread of the property crisis and whether the Chinese state will step in.

wsj.title: Freed Israeli Hostages Still Had Shrapnel in Their Bodies From Oct. 7 Attack
wsj.description: Some of the women were held alone for extended periods and spent eight months in tunnels, an Israeli medical official said.

wsj.title: Suspected Sabotage of Deep-Sea Cable Triggers First NATO-Led Response
wsj.description: The alliance mounted its first coordinated response to a suspected sabotage campaign against critical infrastructure after another cable was severed in the Baltic Sea.

wsj.title: Rwanda-Backed Rebels Enter Congo's Safe-Haven City
wsj.description: Residents of Goma reported gunfire and shelling after rebels overran Congolese troops. U.N. officials estimated that more than one million displaced people were now inside the city.

wsj.title: Cocaine-Funded Gangs Shake Colombia Years After Peace Pact
wsj.description: Attacks by powerful militias against civilians reflect the state’s inaction eight years after a peace accord removed a powerful rebel group from the field.

wsj.title: Italy Supports Saudi Arabia Joining Fighter-Jet Program, PM Meloni Says
wsj.description: The U.K., Italy and Japan have already partnered on the Global Combat Air Programme, which aims to put a new stealth fighter with supersonic capability in the skies by 2035.

wsj.title: Shippers Wary of Red Sea Routes Despite Houthi Pledge to End Targeting
wsj.description: The world’s top three container operators said they fear instability in Gaza and broader regional tensions mean continued danger.

wsj.title: Stocks Sink in Broad AI Rout Sparked by China's DeepSeek
wsj.description: U.S. stocks were mostly lower, with the Nasdaq leading declines as makers of AI infrastructure suffered steep falls, many in the double digits. Nvidia was down 16%.

wsj.title: Comex Gold, Silver Settle Lower
wsj.description: Gold settled 1.4% lower, down two of the past three sessions, and silver fell 2.5%, down three of the past four sessions.

wsj.title: DeepSeek Won't Sink U.S. AI Titans
wsj.description: Panic fueling the selloff of Nvidia, Broadcom and other tech giants is overblown.

wsj.title: Arabica Coffee Prices Hit Record on U.S., Colombia Tariff Spat
wsj.description: Arabica coffee prices hit a record level, as traders digested the withdrawal of President Trump’s threats to impose tariffs and economic sanctions on Colombia.

wsj.title: Swiss franc, Japanese yen Rise as DeepSeek News Boosts Safe Havens
wsj.description: The Yen and Swiss Franc were stronger against the dollar as investors sought safe havens after Chinese start-up DeepSeek’s new AI model hit U.S. tech stocks.

wsj.title: Natural Gas Falls on Shifting Weather Forecasts
wsj.description: U.S. natural gas futures fell after weekend updates took some of the chill out of early February weather forecasts.

wsj.title: Building-Products Distributor QXO Launches Hostile Bid for Beacon
wsj.description: QXO is taking its offer directly to shareholders after being rebuffed on several occasions.

bloomberg.title: Iran War Causes Biggest-Ever Oil Market Disruption, IEA Says
bloomberg.description: The Iran war is causing unprecedented turmoil in oil markets, hitting 7.5% of global supply and an even bigger swath of exports, the International Energy Agency said.

bloomberg.title: JPMorgan, UBS Dropped Hedge Fund Embroiled in HK Probe

bloomberg.title: Stocks Slide as Oil Supply Disruptions Deepen: Markets Wrap
bloomberg.description: Stocks retreated across the world as oil prices kept rising amid widening disruptions to crude transport operations in the Middle East. A gauge of global bonds erased its 2026 advance.

bloomberg.title: Rosebank’s Simon Peckham Aims to ‘Buy, Improve, Sell’ Faster
bloomberg.description: The story of London’s capital markets in recent years has been dominated by overseas firms taking advantage of low UK valuations to snap up businesses. Simon Peckham is pushing in the other direction.

bloomberg.title: Emerging-Currency Options Are Hinting at Lot More Near-Term Pain
bloomberg.description: Traders are bracing for more weakness in emerging-market currencies over the coming month as the Iran war escalates, according to a key options pricing measure.

bloomberg.title: Bond Traders Are Pricing in Bigger Deficits Around the World
bloomberg.description: The yield on the 30-year US Treasury bond is climbing toward 4.9%, part of a global move higher in borrowing costs.

bloomberg.title: Spot Container Rates to Europe Jump as Iran War Strains Capacity
bloomberg.description: The cost to ship goods in containers to Europe from China jumped over the past week as the war in the Mideast disrupts one of the world’s busiest shipping routes and lengthens delivery times.

bloomberg.title: War Is Pushing Iran’s Water Supply to the Brink of Collapse
bloomberg.description: Climate-fueled drought and mismanagement have left the country with a severe water shortage, and war only adds to the strain.

bloomberg.title: Iran War Tests Egypt’s Resilience After Bond Outflows Surge
bloomberg.description: Egyptian assets have been among the Middle East’s worst hit by the Iran war, a major stress test for grueling International Monetary Fund-backed reforms meant to protect against future crises.

aljazeera.description: North Korea face Australia in the Women's Asian Cup quarterfinals, and promise no repeat of protests in China game.

washpost.description: The strike on an Iranian elementary school killed at least 175, many of them children, raising questions as to whether the military’s use of AI-enabled targeting was a factor.

washpost.title: Aid worker killed in drone strike on building used by Congo relief staff
washpost.description: French aid worker Karine Buisset died in the attack. Two others were also killed, according to rebel group M23. Congo’s government and M23 blamed each other.

washpost.title: After Iran assault, Russians say U.S. can’t be trusted in Ukraine talks
washpost.description: As Washington focuses on its push to topple Iran’s government, delaying talks on Russia’s war in Ukraine, some in Moscow say the Kremlin must achieve its goals militarily.

washpost.title: As Israel targets Iran, Gaza’s nascent recovery stalls and Hamas gains strength

washpost.title: UAE, an oasis for business and partying, faces war
washpost.description: Iranian airstrikes have shaken Persian Gulf countries, undermining their reputations as havens of wealth and stability and forcing them to take sides in a war they opposed.
